description: valley of (the son of) hinnom; ge-henna (or ge-hinnom), a valley of jerusalem, used (figuratively) as a name for the place (or state) of everlasting punishment
notes: this word literally refers to the valley of hinnom, south of jerusalem, where the filth and dead animals of the city were cast out and burned, which is a fit symbol of the wicked and their future destruction. this word is transliterated as "gehenna."
